blocks.text: Psalm 85
How lovely is your dwelling place, O Lord of hosts!
Ps 84: Longing for the Temple and a prayer for the Davidic king. A song of Zion, with emphasis on the desire to participate in its worship (cf. Pss 42–43; 63).
1 : Lovely, better, “beloved” as in Isa 5.1; Deut 33.12.
My soul longs, indeed it faints for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh sing for joy to the living God.
Even the sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, at your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.

3 : NRSV wrongly assumes birds’ nests are at the altar, where birds’ nests would never be allowed. The comparison is to a homeless bird finding a home for its young, suggesting: “As a sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, so my home” is at your altars.
Happy are those who live in your house, ever singing your praise. Selah
4 : (also v. 8), see Ps 3.2n.
Happy are those whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways to Zion.
As they go through the valley of Baca they make it a place of springs; the early rain also covers it with pools.
6 : , precise location unknown.
They go from strength to strength; the God of gods will be seen in Zion.
5–7 : The joyous final stage of the journey to Zion.
7 : , a puzzling phrase, perhaps “from rampart to rampart,” the outer and inner gates of Jerusalem.
O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
Behold our shield, O God; look on the face of your anointed.
9 : , the Davidic king played an important role in the ceremonies in Zion.
For a day in your courts is better than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than live in the tents of wickedness.
For the Lord God is a sun and shield; he bestows favor and honor. No good thing does the Lord withhold from those who walk uprightly.
O Lord of hosts, happy is everyone who trusts in you.
